events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
events.description: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. The UN announced the launch of a task force to meet the immense needs. In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the school strike in Iran’s Minab that killed more than 100 children. Stay with us for live updates on this and from UN agencies providing relief. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region

events.title: World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’

events.title: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
events.description: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthquake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake

events.title: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
events.description: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears

events.title: Sir Westmacott challenges Trump rationale as war on Iran sparks 'serious' economic fallout worldwide
events.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Sir Peter Westmacott, distinguished ambassadorial fellow with the Atlantic Council's Europe Center, who was British Ambassador to Turkey, France and the United States. As a senior British diplomat with decades of experience across key transatlantic and Middle Eastern postings, he approaches the current crisis with both concern and analytical caution.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sir Westmacott challenges Trump rationale as war on Iran sparks 'serious' economic fallout worldwide

events.title: Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas
events.description: Football federation president on the run with wife and son Conviction in absentia of wide-ranging corruption charges Authorities in Congo-Brazzaville have applied to Interpol for an international arrest warrant against Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas, the president of the country’s football federation, Fecofoot, after he was convicted of embezzling $1.1m in Fifa funds. Mayolas is on the run with his wife and son after they were all sentenced to life imprisonment this month for embezzling funds provided by world football’s governing body as part of its Covid-19 relief plan in February 2021. As the Guardian revealed last year, that included almost $500,000 earmarked for the Congo women’s team. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas

events.title: Dezi Freeman shot dead by police after seven-month manhunt
events.description: Victoria police commissioner Mike Bush said the shooting was ‘justified’ and brought closure to the families of two police officers allegedly killed by Freeman in Porepunkah in August Who is Dezi Freeman? Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Fugitive Dezi Freeman, the man allegedly responsible for the shooting deaths of two officers at Porepunkah, has been killed after a seven-month manhunt in rural Victoria. The Victoria police chief commissioner, Mike Bush, confirmed a man was fatally shot by police shortly after 8.30am on Monday, after an hours-long standoff in which he failed to surrender peacefully at a property at Thologolong in the state’s north-east. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dezi Freeman shot dead by police after seven-month manhunt

events.title: One Lebanese soldier killed, Israel expands buffer zone in southern Lebanon
events.description: Israel renewed its bombardment of Beirut's southern suburbs on March 30 while continuing air strikes on Lebanon's south, one of which targeted an army checkpoint and killed a soldier. In south Lebanon, where state media reported a series of Israeli air strikes, the Lebanese army said one of its soldiers was killed and others wounded in an attack on one of its checkpoints in the Tyre region. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot tells us more from the ground in Beirut
events.ticker_label: 🪖 One Lebanese soldier killed, Israel expands buffer zone in southern Lebanon

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes
events.description: Further attacks have been reported across the Middle East as the war enters a second month, with two more Indonesian peacekeepers killed in Southern Lebanon on Monday, following the death of a fellow ‘blue helmet’ a day earlier. On the diplomatic front, the UN has announced a taskforce to restore the flow of fertilizer and aid through the Strait of Hormuz, while the UN's atomic watchdog confirms an attack on a heavy water facility at Khondab in Iran. Stay with us for live updates on this and UN agencies. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes

events.title: Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns
events.description: The intensifying conflict in the Persian Gulf “has triggered one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times,” the Chief Economist with the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns

events.title: China’s ‘teapot’ oil refineries keep economy brewing – but surging crude prices leave them strained
events.description: The factories, which buy cheap crude and turn it into fuel, are struggling as higher oil prices threaten their razor-sharp margins The towns that are the bulwark of China’s energy security can, at a moment of global crisis , appear deceptively quiet. Trucks carrying oil trundle along wide-open highways that have little traffic, while a few boarded-up shops in crumbling low-rise buildings hint at a long-forgotten local buzz. A ramshackle noodle shop serving hand-pulled ribbons of dough was empty at lunchtime, save for a few construction workers and a teacher watching videos on Douyin, the social media platform, with his meal. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China’s ‘teapot’ oil refineries keep economy brewing – but surging crude prices leave them strained

events.title: 'Warning signs were all there' before deadly DC mid-air crash, former air traffic controller says
events.description: A former Reagan National controller says safety warnings went unheeded for years before the Jan. 2025 midair crash that killed 67 near the Potomac.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Warning signs were all there' before deadly DC mid-air crash, former air traffic controller says

events.title: Father of slain 20-year-old killed by illegal immigrant issues stark warning after Sheridan Gorman killing
events.description: Joe Abraham warns more deaths will follow in Illinois after his daughter Katie and Loyola student Sheridan Gorman were allegedly killed by illegal immigrants.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Father of slain 20-year-old killed by illegal immigrant issues stark warning after Sheridan Gorman killing

events.title: Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida
events.description: More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report that called for greater action towards accountability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida

events.title: World News in Brief: 273 million out of school, deadly attacks on Ukraine, migratory species in danger
events.description: The number of children and young people out of school worldwide has climbed for the seventh consecutive year, reaching 273 million, according to a new report from the UN education agency, UNESCO.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns
events.description: It’s day 26 of war in the Middle East. The UN chief says with the conflict now totally out of control, diplomacy must prevail. Ongoing strikes in Israel and Iran have included intensifying Israeli attacks against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, while some US troops are heading to the region, the Pentagon has confirmed. Meanwhile, Iran has told the UN maritime agency that the Strait of Hormuz remains open to “non-hostile” ships not associated with the US and Israel. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns

events.title: Distancing himself from Trump and a badly waged war in Iran might be the smartest move Albanese can make
events.description: The US president thumbs his nose at concerns about the most serious energy crisis in global history. But Australia, like other countries, is paying a high price Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gently, about 10 days ago, Anthony Albanese tried to send Donald Trump a message about the escalating war in Iran . In a Hobart radio interview, the prime minister said the US had achieved its original justifications and should bring hostilities in the Middle East to an end. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Distancing himself from Trump and a badly waged war in Iran might be the smartest move Albanese can make

events.title: Israel legalises death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
events.description: Israel's parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted on terror charges of carrying out deadly attacks on Israelis. The law, which also allows the courts to impose the death penalty on Israeli citizens, has been harshly criticised by Israeli and Palestinian rights groups.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel legalises death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis

events.title: Three UN peacekeepers killed in south Lebanon in 24 hours amid Israel-Hezbollah conflict
events.description: The UN said on Monday that two members of its peacekeeping force were killed in an explosion in south Lebanon, with a third being killed in a separate blast the previous day. Here's what to know about how the Blue Helmets are involved in the current conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: UN chief calls for immediate release of 118 detained UN staff
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for the immediate release of the 118 UN staff currently detained worldwide as attacks against the United Nations increase with 179 personnel arrested or detained last year alone.

events.title: Most gen Z fathers in Australia believe it’s solely their job to provide financially, research finds
events.description: Survey also found 65% of gen Z think ‘things are better if men do paid work and women do care work’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Younger fathers are more likely to cling to outdated ideas that frame men as the money earners and women as caregivers, new research has found. The Australian State of the World’s Fathers report is based on a global survey of 8,000 parents, with 533 from Australia. Continue reading...

events.title: An in-depth look at Israel's new death penalty law
events.description: The new law, introduced by Israel's far-right government, was passed by parliament Monday night. It imposes the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of fatal attacks.

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.title: Air Canada CEO to resign after backlash to video tribute of pilots killed in crash
events.description: Michael Rousseau faced mockery for speaking English and not French while addressing fatal LaGuardia airport crash The head of Canada’s largest airline is stepping down after his video tribute to pilots killed in a fatal collision became a public relations nightmare for Air Canada, prompting a wave of mockery and indignation at him from both the public and politicians for not speaking French. Air Canada’s CEO, Michael Rousseau, will retire by the end of the third quarter of 2026, the company said on Monday. He will continue to lead the company and serve on the board of directors until that time, the carrier said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Air Canada CEO to resign after backlash to video tribute of pilots killed in crash

events.title: Asian economies scramble to find solutions to war's impact on oil markets
events.description: Iran continues to cut off the strait of Hormuz as it retaliates for the US and Israel's campaign against the Islamic Republic. The strategy has been effective as it piles pressure on the United States to the end the war, with global energy prices continuing to soar. Over 80% of oil and gas typically passing through the Strait of Hormuz heads for Asia.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Asian economies scramble to find solutions to war's impact on oil markets

events.title: Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns
events.description: Malcolm Turnbull asks defence department official what Australia would do if the promised Virginia-class and Aukus-class submarines don’t arrive Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons the Aukus deal with the US and UK, a senior defence official has warned, declining to publicly countenance an alternative plan if Australia’s promised nuclear-powered fleet does not arrive under Australian command. “Defence has been directed to pursue Aukus and we are pursuing Aukus and that’s our plan. I would not venture into the space about ‘Plan B’ or ‘Plan C’,” defence department deputy secretary, Hugh Jeffrey, told a Sovereignty and Security Forum in Canberra on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns

events.title: Australians may not see cheaper fuel for weeks despite Labor’s excise cuts
events.description: Flow-on effect will depend on how quickly service stations sell more expensive fuel, experts warn, leaving Easter travel plans up in the air Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians expecting relief from punishing fuel prices in time for Easter travel are set to be disappointed, with the industry predicting the effects of Labor temporarily halving the excise will take days or weeks to reach some bowsers around the country. The halving of the fuel excise, which begins on Wednesday and lasts until the end of June, means the federal government will now collect 26.3c from every litre over the next three months instead of 52.6c a litre. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australians may not see cheaper fuel for weeks despite Labor’s excise cuts

events.title: 48-hour curfew imposed after attack on bar in Nigerian city
events.description: The raid led to revenge attacks in Jos, with 22 reported dead so far in one of the most volatile parts of the country.

events.title: Kyiv useful to US, Gulf at critical time: Drone interceptors 'more effective than Patriot missiles'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Samuel Ramani, Associate Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute. According to Dr. Ramani, President Zelensky’s outreach to Gulf states reflects a calculated effort to reposition Ukraine within a shifting global order, where military innovation, energy politics, and diplomatic alignment intersect. This repositioning serves multiple objectives: strengthening ties with ambivalent Gulf actors, reinforcing Ukraine’s strategic value to the United States, and attracting much-needed investment into its defense and technology sectors.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kyiv useful to US, Gulf at critical time: Drone interceptors 'more effective than Patriot missiles'

events.title: Trump appears to relax oil blockade on Cuba as Russian tanker arrives
events.description: US president says he has ‘no problem’ with countries sending oil to Cuba, in potential lifeline to island nation Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump appears to have relaxed his blockade on fuel-starved Cuba after a Russian tanker reached the Caribbean island carrying 100,000 tonnes of crude. Russia’s transport ministry said the Anatoly Kolodkin tanker arrived at the port of Matanzas on Cuba’s northern coast on Monday to deliver the crisis-hit country’s first such cargo in more than three months. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war
events.description: US president says he will host Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit later this year Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump will meet Xi Jinping in May during the US president’s first visit to China in eight years, a closely watched trip that had been postponed due to the Iran war. Trump was initially slated to travel next week, but will now visit Beijing on 14 and 15 May, he wrote in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday. Trump said he would host the Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit in Washington later this year. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: ‘Succulent Chinese meal’ speech added to Australia’s National Film and Sound Archive
events.description: Jack Karlson’s rallying cry of ‘democracy manifest’ added to national collection of sound recordings that hold historical, cultural and aesthetic significance Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Thirty-five years ago, when Jack Karlson was hauled into a police car outside a Chinese restaurant in Queensland, he couldn’t have known his bombastic speech would be watched by millions around the world, become a meme and now, be preserved for ever in Australia’s National Film and Sound Archive. Karlson’s declaration – “Gentlemen, this is democracy manifest! … What is the charge? Eating a meal? A succulent Chinese meal?” – is one of nine pieces of audio that have been added to the NFSA’s Sounds of Australia collection this year, along with a pedestrian crossing signal and Missy Higgins ’ 2004 hit Scar. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘Succulent Chinese meal’ speech added to Australia’s National Film and Sound Archive

events.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela in significant thawing of relations
events.description: Resumption of diplomatic operations come three months after former president Maduro was abducted Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US government is resuming operations at its embassy in Venezuela, the state department announced on Monday, nearly three months since former president Nicolás Maduro was abducted from the country and locked up in the US. The resumption of US diplomatic operations in Venezuela marks a significant step in the US-Venezuela relationship, as the Trump administration begins to work closely with the government of Delcy Rodríguez , the acting president who replaced Maduro after his forcible ousting by US troops. Rodríguez was Maduro’s vice-president. Continue reading...

events.title: Brent crude hits $116 a barrel as Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ Iran’s oil wells and export hub
events.description: Investors nervous over escalation of Middle East conflict as US president says he wants to ‘take the oil in Iran’ Business live – latest news The price of oil hit nearly $117 (£89) a barrel on Monday as Donald Trump threatened to “blow up” and “completely obliterate” Iranian electricity plants, oil wells and its export hub Kharg Island if it did not agree to a deal. Brent crude rose after Trump wrote on his social media platform Truth Social that if a deal was not agreed and the strait of Hormuz was not reopened, the US would take further action. Continue reading...

events.title: Capital punishment: Which countries use the death penalty?
events.description: Israel is debating the approval of a death penalty bill that targets Palestinians. Although the number of countries carrying out executions is declining, the total number of executions is on the rise.

events.title: Ukraine signs defence agreements with Gulf Nations eager to fend off Iranian drones
events.description: The Ukrainian president has hailed "historic" defence agreements with several Middle Eastern countries after his visit to the region last week. Volodymyr Zelensky has been seeking to capitalise on his military's drone-fighting prowess as countries like the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia continue to come under attack from Iranian drones and missiles. The president has insisted that any deal should also benefit Ukraine -- which is seeking expensive air defence missiles.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine signs defence agreements with Gulf Nations eager to fend off Iranian drones

events.title: Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage
events.description: Uganda's military chief Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba claims Uganda could enter the Iran-Israel war on Israel's side, posting a series of pro-Israel statements on X this week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage

events.title: Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ desalination plants in Iran if no deal reached
events.description: Legal expert says targeting civilian sites would be 'collective punishment', which is prohibited under laws of war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ desalination plants in Iran if no deal reached

events.title: Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead
events.description: Finnish authorities are investigating two drones that crashed in southern Finland. Kyiv apologized for the incident, with Ukraine's Zelenskyy saying he and Finland's Stubb are on the same page.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Finnish police say stray Ukrainian drone carried warhead

events.title: Bab al-Mandab strait: Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, raising fears over key trade route
events.description: As Iran’s Houthi allies enter the conflict, Iran has threatened to disrupt shipping in the Bab al-Mandab Strait – a strategic waterway linking the Gulf of Aden to the Red Sea and the Suez Canal – with the help of its Houthi allies in Yemen. While disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz primarily affect Asia, blocking access to the Gulf of Aden could affect European energy supplies and imports from Asia.

events.title: South Lebanon: Locals remain as war rages on
events.description: 🇱🇧 "Nowhere is safe." Mere kilometres from the #Israel-#Lebanon border, FRANCE 24 accompanied a humanitarian aid convoy headed to the town of #Marjayoun. For the thousands that still remain in the region, convoys like this are their last resort for essential necessities. Our reporters Melina Huet and Achraf Abid bring you this story.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Lebanon: Locals remain as war rages on

events.title: Hundreds of mourners gather in pouring rain for funerals of Lebanon journalists
events.description: Hundreds of mourners turned out in the pouring rain in Beirut for the funerals of three journalists killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hundreds of mourners gather in pouring rain for funerals of Lebanon journalists

events.title: Pakistan says it will host US-Iran talks, while Iran warns US ground troops would be 'set on fire'

events.title: Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say
events.description: Israeli officials say Iran's internet blackout is hiding war damage, jamming Starlink, and stopping civilians from organizing amid mounting regime pressure.

events.title: Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows

events.title: Avi Lewis, elected to lead Canada’s New Democratic party, promises ‘NDP comeback’
events.description: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. A record number of members voted in the three-day NDP leadership convention, giving Lewis a first-ballot win that underscored widespread support. Lewis pledged to convert the “tremendous momentum” of the convention into an “NDP comeback”. Continue reading...

events.title: While Trump vows Iran deal on the way, 'evidence points to another direction'
events.description: US President Donald Trump threatened on March 30 to destroy Iran's crude export hub of Kharg Island, along with its oil wells and power plants, unless Tehran quickly accepted a peace deal. He also claimed that the United States was in 'serious discussions' with 'a more reasonable regime' in Tehran. 'While Trump points in one direction, empirical evidence seems to point the other way' says FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.

events.title: 500 groups with $3B in revenues are behind the #NoKings protests and communist call for 'revolution'
events.description: A nationwide protest billed as grassroots is supported by a network of roughly 500 organizations with billions in combined revenue, including groups tied to Democratic advocacy circles and socialist activist movements.
events.ticker_label: 💥 500 groups with $3B in revenues are behind the #NoKings protests and communist call for 'revolution'

events.title: Over 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted, but a critical vulnerability is growing, report warns

events.title: Cardiff City damages claim over Emiliano Sala death dismissed

events.title: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs

events.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
events.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.description: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
events.description: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to develop north of Saudi Arabia. This, in turn, drew moist tropical air from the Indian Ocean and triggered intense storms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia

events.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
events.description: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by giving perpetrators new tools to control and silence women and girls, and influence boys. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia refuses to say how many Chinese nationals are arriving by boat, saying it may damage bilateral relations
events.description: Exclusive: Indonesia reports growing number of attempts by Chinese nationals to organise boat journeys, as Australian authorities refuse to reveal details Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has refused to reveal how many Chinese nationals have arrived in Australia by boat since 2024, saying that disclosing the figure may harm relations with other countries. However, reports by Indonesian police show that there has been a consistent trend of Chinese nationals attempting to reach Australia through Indonesia as an alternative to “zouxian”, or “walking the line” – the illegal migration route through Central America to the US via the Darién Gap between Colombia and Panama. Continue reading...

events.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey

events.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message

events.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.description: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: Germany: Stranded Humpback whale has 'barely moved' in Baltic Sea, is reacting less to stimuli
events.description: The stranded whale barely moved overnight, despite a period with higher water levels presenting an opportunity. Rescuers said it was reacting less to stimuli and breathing much less frequently — signs of deterioration.

events.title: Israel suspends battalion over attack on CNN journalists
events.description: In a rare move, the Israeli military has suspended an entire reserve battalion following an attack on CNN journalists in the occupied West Bank.

events.title: Zelensky hails 'historic' defence agreements with Gulf states
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Monday said he signed "historic" agreements with countries in the Middle East last week, including sharing Ukrainian expertise on downing drones as Iranian strikes continue to target Gulf countries. Ukraine has previously proposed an exchange of its drone interceptors for the Gulf's air defence missiles.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelensky hails 'historic' defence agreements with Gulf states

events.title: Israel's proposed 'discriminatory' death penalty plans criticised by UK and allies

events.title: Missing banker’s remains ID’d — again — after beach discovery in decades-old unsolved mystery

events.title: Teen girl gunned down in posh Chicago enclave as police rush to nab her killer

events.title: Executed killer’s chilling claim about what she felt during pickax murders still haunts retired FBI agent
events.description: Retired FBI profiler Candice DeLong says Karla Faye Tucker's troubled childhood and drug use doomed her long before the 1983 pickax murders that sent her to death row.

events.title: Body found in search for missing American Airlines flight attendant in Colombia: mayor

events.title: Russian man who assaulted woman during Barron Trump FaceTime call sentenced to 4 years

events.title: Germany hosts Syria's Al-Sharaa despite human rights issues
events.description: Germany is keen for Syrian refugees to return home. The visit by interim Syrian President al-Sharaa highlights shifting policies on repatriation and regional stability — despite accusations of human rights violations.

events.description: Brazil has a nationwide program that lets prisoners cut their sentences by reading books and proving they understood them

events.description: What could possibly go wrong? As Donald Trump repeats threats of a Venezuela-style seizing of Tehran’s oil exports, as US assault troops get in position, the price of oil blowing past 110 dollars a barrel, and as markets take it at face value that the Pentagon's planning to make a grab for Kharg Island - the hub for nearly 90-percent of Iranian crude exports...

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Goodbye Graaff-Reinet: South African town’s name change stirs racial tensions
events.description: Minister’s decision to ditch town’s colonial-era identity and honour anti-apartheid activist divides residents A South African town is divided over changing its name from the colonial-era Graaff-Reinet to Robert Sobukwe, after the anti-apartheid activist, in a debate that has inflamed racial tensions. Petitions have been signed, rival marches held and a formal letter of complaint sent to the sports, arts and culture minister, Gayton McKenzie, who approved the name change on 6 February. Continue reading...

events.title: US lawmakers express support for stalled Taiwan special defense budget
events.description: U.S. lawmakers visiting Taiwan say the United States supports efforts by Taiwan's government to pass a $40 billion special defense budget that is being stalled in the opposition-controlled parliament

events.title: UN resolution urges reparations for slavery’s ‘historical wrongs’

events.title: Russian oil tanker reaches Cuba after Trump appears to loosen blockade
events.description: The tanker has reached Cuban waters just hours after Donald Trump said he had "no problem" with it.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
events.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

events.title: Paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse stolen from Italian museum
events.description: Three ⁠paintings by ​French masters Pierre-Auguste Renoir, Paul Cézanne and Henri ​Matisse, reportedly worth an estimated $10 million in total, have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy, ​police ‌said on Monday.

events.title: New Paris mayor vows to tackle systemic abuse in after-school programmes
events.description: Paris' new mayor Emmanuel Grégoire officially started on March 30. His first promise has been to tackle child abuse in after-school programmes, an issue that was at the centre of the latest municipal elections. Emmanuel Grégoire was himself a victim of abuse in an after-school programme, which he made public last September. The issue is not specific to Paris - cases have appeared all over France. FRANCE 24's Solange Mougin looks into the issue.

events.title: Over 2 dozen children among 33 bodies pulled from Kenyan mass grave: authorities

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.description: Three paintings by French masters Cezanne, Renoir, and Matisse have been taken from a museum near the Italian city of Parma.

events.title: US not 'postured' for another Iraq 2003: Iran maintains vast, 'high-quality military capability'
events.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Michael Knights, Adjunct Fellow at The Washington Institute and Head of Research at Horizon Engage. He examines the evolving military dynamics between the US and Iran, focusing on the operational possibilities available to US forces and the structural resilience of Iran’s defence strategy. According to Knights, decisive, large-scale invasions are highly unlikely. He argues that while the US possesses the capacity to execute targeted missions, Iran remains a formidable adversary, with a deeply layered and decentralised military system.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US not 'postured' for another Iraq 2003: Iran maintains vast, 'high-quality military capability'

events.description: On the 50th Land Day, Al Jazeera’s Hala Al Shami looks into what happened in Palestine on March 30, 1976.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: US embassy in Mexico prompts outrage with AI video promoting ‘self-deportation’
events.description: AI-generated footage depicts group of men performing a corrido, singing phrases including ‘return to your roots’ An AI-generated video from the US embassy in Mexico encouraging migrants to “self-deport” has sparked disbelief and outrage online. The video posted this week on official embassy social media accounts depicts a group of men wearing black caps and sporting tattoos performing a kind of traditional Mexican ballad known as a corrido . Continue reading...

events.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
events.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon

events.title: Trump is weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
events.description: President Trump said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

events.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
events.description: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

events.title: Sixty-six ways to fix Germany's expensive health care system
events.description: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

events.title: Germany news: Merz and Syrian president talk refugee returns
events.description: As Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa visits Berlin, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says about 80% of Syrians living in Germany are expected to return home. Meanwhile, fears are mounting about inflation. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany news: Merz and Syrian president talk refugee returns

events.title: EU seeks new strategy amid Sahel 'influence' rivalries
events.description: Following the 2022-2023 coups in Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso, the junta regimes turned to Russia, China and even the US. Now, the EU wants to invest more in West Africa's security and growth to forge closer ties.

events.title: How North Korea is using Belarus to deepen ties with Russia
events.description: A rare visit to North Korea by Belarus' strongman leader Alexander Lukashenko is part of Kim's broader push to deepen its ties with Russia's sphere of influence.

events.title: Myanmar clears path for junta leader to become president
events.description: Min Aung Hlaing has been replaced by a close ally as military commander. He will now face a three-way vote for president by a parliament mostly loyal to the army.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader accepts Xi's invite to visit China
events.description: Cheng Li-wun's visit to China, expected to take place in April, would be the first by a sitting leader of Taiwan's main opposition party in a decade.

events.title: Artemis II: A new space race to the moon?
events.description: 🚀 🌕 After months of delay, the #ArtemisII mission is set to take to the skies for a lunar flyby on Wednesday. Though the short-term goal is to bring humankind back to the #moon, there's a lot more at stake behind the scenes. FRANCE 24's Tanishk Saha explains ▶️ #NASA #Space #Artemis

events.title: France hands captain of suspected Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker one-year jail sentence
events.description: A French court on Monday sentenced in absentia the captain of a suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker to one year in jail for failing to comply with orders to stop his ship. The vessel, which the French navy boarded in September before releasing the vessel, was thought to be transporting Russian oil in violation of Western sanctions.

events.title: Russian oil arrives in Cuba following Trump turnaround on Cuban blockade
events.description: A Russian oil tanker has reached Cuba, bringing a much-needed shipment to the oil-starved nation. The shipment of oil would be the first to Havana since January, when US forces abducted Venezuelan president and Cuban ally Nicolas Maduro in a raid. This comes after US President Donald Trump signalled to a softening of the blockade against the island nation. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle looks into this change of course from Trump, and what could be behind it.

events.title: Music show: Arlo Parks going dancefloor, Italian electro pop and Breton psych rock
events.description: This week on arts24 we welcome on set Italian electro pop artist Gaia Banfi, whose new album "La Maccaia" takes us to the Gulf of Genoa. We also have a chat with psych rock Breton band Komodor and check out Arlo Parks' long-awaited third album "Ambiguous Desire".

events.title: Paintings 'worth millions' stolen in three-minute heist
events.description: Three famous paintings worth millions have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy.

events.title: Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official
events.description: A man has appeared in a Dublin court after being charged with the murder of a former Sinn Fein official who was shot dead 20 years ago.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official

events.title: Fugitive accused of killing two police officers 'shot dead'
events.description: A fugitive accused of killing two police officers is believed to have been shot dead, Australian police have said.

events.title: Russia kicks out British diplomat accused of spying
events.description: Russia has kicked out a British diplomat that Moscow has accused of spying.

events.title: Quarantined astronauts take part in Q&A with days to go until historic moon mission
events.description: The countdown is on until NASA's first moon mission in more than half a century, and the quarantined astronauts who'll be travelling into space have taken part in a Q&A.

events.title: Egyptian diplomat Nabil Fahmy appointed as new Arab League chief
events.description: Arab foreign ministers have picked veteran Egyptian diplomat Nabil Fahmy to lead the Arab League for the next five years

events.title: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say
events.description: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say

events.title: Russia expels British diplomat over spying allegations
events.description: Russia has expelled a British diplomat it accuses of spying

events.title: North Korea conducts engine test for missile capable of targeting US mainland
events.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has observed a test of a high-thrust, solid-fuel engine for weapons
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea conducts engine test for missile capable of targeting US mainland

events.title: A stranded whale in Germany’s Baltic Sea weakens as hopes of its return to the Atlantic fade
events.description: A stranded humpback whale in Germany’s Baltic Sea looks weaker, and experts fear it won't be able to find its way back to the Atlantic

events.title: Sea mines clearance: A new dimension of difficulty
events.description: Clearing mines laid at sea can be an “extremely challenging and very dangerous” undertaking according to a UN mines expert.

events.title: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
events.description: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

events.title: 109 spring breakers hauled off in one wild house party raid—cops turn beach bash into a perp parade
events.description: Over 140 spring breakers were arrested at two Gulf Shores, Alabama, parties, including one where a person allegedly poured beer on an officer's head.

events.title: US reopens embassy in Caracas, citing progress after Maduro extraction
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Caracas has formally resumed operations for the first time since 2019, a key milestone in Trump's three-phase plan for Venezuela.

events.title: DHS criticizes plea deal that could free migrant after guilty plea in teen assault case
events.description: DHS slammed a plea deal for Contreras-Suarez, a transgender illegal alien who pleaded guilty to raping a 14-year-old in NYC, calling it disgraceful.

events.title: Vacation rental near Yosemite allegedly used to secretly record guests, 4,000 illegal files found
events.description: A Madera County man faces felony charges after detectives allegedly found over 4,000 child sexual abuse files at a vacation rental near Yosemite.

events.title: ’90 Day Fiance’ alum’s boyfriend beats Boca Bash attempted murder case he rips as ‘ridiculous’
events.description: Cole Goldberg was acquitted of attempted murder charges tied to a 2022 Boca Bash incident involving a "90 Day Fiancé" personality after a bench trial.
events.ticker_label: 💥 ’90 Day Fiance’ alum’s boyfriend beats Boca Bash attempted murder case he rips as ‘ridiculous’

events.title: Gas thief creeps into learning center, drains van serving students with disabilities
events.description: A thief was caught on camera siphoning gas from a transport van at a Clovis, California, school serving nearly 190 students who have intellectual disabilities.

events.title: North Korean laborers describe brutal forced labor in Russia: 'Working like a cow, earning nothing'
events.description: A North Korean forced labor survivor describes earning $800 a month but keeping only $10, with passports seized and no way to leave Russian work sites.

events.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
events.description: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
